SetEnvVarsRequest.description:
Request body for PUT /env_vars — sets (upserts) one or more environment variables.

# Lifecycle model

Environment variables are written to `/etc/environment` inside a VM **once
at boot time** via a vsock `WriteFile` request. They are **not** live-synced
to running VMs. This is intentional: VMs are ephemeral (create → use →
destroy/branch), so env var changes naturally take effect on the next VM.

The `replace` flag exists so callers can atomically express "I want exactly
these variables and nothing else" without having to DELETE each stale key
individually. Without it, the only way to remove a variable from future VMs
is a separate `DELETE /env_vars/{key}` call per key.

replace.description:
If true, delete all existing variables before writing the new set.
This gives "set exactly these vars" semantics. Default: false (upsert).

vars.description:
Key-value pairs to set. Keys must be valid shell identifiers
(`^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$`, max 256 chars). Values max 8192 chars.

When `replace` is false (default): existing keys are overwritten, keys
not mentioned are left untouched (upsert semantics).

When `replace` is true: all existing variables are deleted first, then
only the provided vars are stored (replace-all semantics).

is_public.description: Whether the repository should be publicly visible

TagInfo.description: Tag information returned in list and get operations

commit_id.description: The commit ID this tag currently points to

description.description: Optional description of what this tag represents

description.description: Optional new description for the tag. Send `null` to clear.

UpdateTagRequest.description:
Request body for PATCH /api/v1/commit_tags/{tag_name}

For `description`:
- Field absent from JSON → don't change the description
- Field present as `null` → clear the description
- Field present as `"text"` → set the description to "text"

commit_id.description: Optional new commit ID to move the tag to

description.description: Optional new description for the tag. Send `null` to clear an existing description.

grandparent_commit_id.description: The commit that this commit's parent VM was started from, if any. Intended to optimize traversing the commit tree.

is_public.description: Whether this commit is publicly accessible (readable/restorable by anyone).

parent_vm_id.description: The VM that this commit was created from, if any.

commit_id.description: The UUID of the newly-created commit

VmCreateVmConfig.description: Struct representing configuration options common to all VMs

image_name.description: The filesystem base image name. Currently, must be 'default'

kernel_name.description: The kernel name. Currently, must be 'default.bin'

vcpu_count.description: How many vCPUs to allocate to this VM (and its children)

fs_size_mib.description: The new disk size in MiB. Must be strictly greater than the current size.

ssh_port.description: The SSH port that will be DNAT'd to the VM's netns (and, in turn, to its TAP device)

ssh_private_key.description: Private SSH key in stringified OpenSSH format
